headline: Dengue Deaths Reach 148 in 2025 Despite RM380 Million Spent on Control
context: Dengue fatalities hit 148 in 2025 with 125,000 reported cases despite significant government vector control spending.

cards.big: 148 dengue deaths and 125,000 cases recorded in 2025 despite RM380 million control budget
cards.sub: Malaysia remains one of the highest dengue-burden countries in Southeast Asia

cards.big: Fogging operations cover only 45% of identified hotspot areas within 24 hours of notification
cards.sub: Delayed response allows mosquito breeding cycles to continue during critical periods

cards.big: Wolbachia mosquito release program covers only 8 districts out of 154 nationwide
cards.sub: Proven biological control method remains a small-scale pilot after 7 years

cards.big: Singapore reduced dengue incidence 70% through integrated surveillance and community engagement
cards.sub: Real-time monitoring with rapid response achieves better outcomes than periodic spraying

cards.big: Spending millions on fogging while underfunding proven alternatives is expensive inefficiency
Should Malaysia scale up Wolbachia nationally and phase out conventional fogging programs
